title: Whole-state reread after a burden lands (daee-epistemics import 2)
note: Two coupled burdens: B1 (migrate the store) and B2 (rollback rehearsal, whose plan depends on the OLD store). Resolving B1 invalidates B2's risk-acceptance basis: the reread after B1 lands re-dispositions B2 to unresolved instead of letting the episode close on the locally repaired item. The ledger below is the POST-REREAD state: B2 is honestly unresolved and, per Definition 13 (machine rule: validate_cross_record_semantics), no closure claim exists. The pre-reread false-closure variant (closure_claim asserted over an unresolved residual) is negative fixture N11 and must be rejected.

claims.proposition: all rows live in the new store and consumers read from it
claims.success_surface: read-path metrics on the new store

residuals.description: move data and switch consumers to the new store
rehearse rollback; the accepted-risk record was written when rollback meant 'repoint to the old store' — B1's completion decommissioned that path, so the acceptance basis is void
